title: Add an early Mihai voice note around day 4-5
description: A short personal voice note from Mihai between day 3 (founder video) and day 6 (impact deep-dive). Fills a gap where the Angel has had one big moment but nothing personal yet. Reinforces that Mihai is genuinely tracking their arrival.

title: Trigger recruiter peer welcome at day 2
description: If the Angel was referred by a current Angel, the recruiter should send a short personal message at day 2. Gabi or Alice triggers the nudge. Peer validation at this moment converts anxiety into belonging.

title: Seed 1-2 Angel-to-Angel intros in week 2
description: Gabi identifies 1-2 existing Angels with obvious overlap (city, industry, background) and makes a warm intro by day 10-14. First peer connection is the strongest predictor of long-term retention.

title: Design 1-2 agency moments in week 1
description: Give the new Angel a small task with visible stakes: vote on a program option, suggest a speaker, name a recipient. Agency creates ownership. Without it the first 30 days are passive consumption.

title: Thread the four claims through stewardship
description: The claims (200 Angels by Dec 31 / 2050 Romania / $1B impact) are powerful but they disappear after the pitch. Weave them into at least 3 stewardship touches so the Angel internalizes the mission arc, not just the year-1 gift.

title: Build a specific-impact lookup per tier per quarter
description: Each Angel should be able to see exactly what their tier funded last quarter: a named scholarship, a specific event, a program moment. Generic impact reports erode trust. Specific ones compound it.

title: Add a physical artifact (signed postcard minimum)
description: A hand-addressed postcard signed by Mihai arrives in week 2. Even a minimal physical artifact outperforms digital-only onboarding in retention studies. Archangel+ gets a heavier item.

title: Make the cultivate-to-steward handoff intentional
description: Mihai names Gabi explicitly in the day-3 founder video: 'Gabi is your Angel Liaison — she's here for everything from here.' Without the explicit handoff, Angels don't know who to contact and Gabi loses authority.

title: Add a tier-aware touch in the sequence
description: One moment in the 30-day sequence calls out the Angel's specific tier by name and explains what it unlocks. Makes the tier feel like an identity, not just a price point.

title: Add running 200-counter to the welcome kit
description: Include the Angel's place in the founding cohort: 'You are #117 of the first 200.' Scarcity and founding-cohort identity are powerful motivators. The number should be accurate at send time.

title: Backup human for Gabi + automation safety net
description: If Gabi is unavailable for more than 48 hours, Mihaela steps in. Alice monitors for missed touches (day 1 through day 30 triggers) and flags anything that didn't fire. No Angel should fall through the gap silently.

title: Add 2-3 secondary engagement signals beyond Angel Spaces
description: Day-30 success = first Angel Spaces interaction. But that's a lagging indicator. Add 2-3 leading signals in weeks 1-2: WhatsApp reply rate, link clicks in welcome kit, RSVP to any event. Lets Gabi intervene before day 30.

title: Stress-test cadence with first 10 Angels before full rollout
description: Run the 30-day sequence manually with the first 10 Angels (or simulate it). Find the broken steps before turning on automation for everyone. One broken automated touch at scale is harder to fix than at 10.

title: Schedule quarterly welcome kit refresh
description: The welcome kit goes stale within one year: dates, impact numbers, and program references all age. Assign Madi a quarterly review slot (January, April, July, October) to update the kit before the next cohort.

title: Prioritize NB to GlobalRomania integration
description: Manual matching between NationBuilder and GlobalRomania (Circle) is the single biggest operational risk. A new Angel who doesn't appear in Circle within 48 hours falls out of the day-1 community touch. Programmatic sync eliminates this class of failure.

title: Build an explicit tier-override path for Archangel and Legacy tiers
description: The 30-day sequence is designed for the standard Angel tier. Archangel ($5K) and Legacy Builder ($10K) Angels expect a meaningfully different experience. Define what changes: different physical artifact, direct Mihai call on day 3 instead of video, different Circle space.

title: Build a recruiter-side nudge system and fallback if recruiter goes silent
description: The recruiter peer-welcome at day 2 only works if the recruiter responds. Alice monitors for non-response after 24 hours and escalates to Gabi to cover. Without the fallback, the warmest possible touch is also the most fragile.

title: Peer-to-peer Angel mentorship matching
description: Longer-term benefit: Angels with 1+ year of tenure mentor new Angels in the same industry or city. Deepens peer bonds, gives senior Angels a leadership role, and creates a natural retention hook for year-2 renewal.

title: Annual Angel Council
description: 10-15 Angels selected (rotating) for real strategic input: program decisions, grant priorities, new benefit design. Not a rubber stamp. Angels who feel heard stay. This is the highest-retention benefit we can build at low cost.

title: Family and next-generation benefits
description: Magic Camp priority slot, Via Transilvanica family day, young-leaders program visibility for Angel children. Anchors the Angel's identity as a Romanian legacy builder, not just a donor.

title: Career and business reciprocity
description: Angel job board, warm intros within the Angel network, peer mentorship for business challenges. Turns the Angel network into a two-way value exchange. Most diaspora giving programs are one-directional — this differentiates RUF.

title: Trust and accountability benefits
description: Quarterly transparency briefings, CFO Q&A access, program audit visibility. Angels who see the books trust the mission. This benefit costs almost nothing and eliminates the #1 attrition signal (feeling like a passive ATM).

title: Rituals and moments
description: Angel Day on December 1, an annual induction ceremony for the newest cohort, anniversary recognition at year 1 and year 3. Rituals create belonging. Without them the Angel relationship is purely transactional.

title: Personal Angel page
description: A per-Angel URL (e.g. globalromania.com/angels/ion-popescu) showing their cumulative impact story: years active, funds contributed, programs touched, people helped. Shareable. Becomes a social identity artifact.

title: Time bank — Angels offer expertise hours
description: Angels can contribute expertise (legal, marketing, tech, finance) to a brokered pool that RUF matches to program needs. Gives non-financial value a formal channel. Especially powerful for Angels who give time and money.

title: Pre-decision visibility for higher tiers
description: Archangel and Legacy Builder Angels receive strategic briefings before major announcements: new programs, big hires, capital decisions. 'No surprises' is itself a benefit. Makes higher-tier giving feel like real partnership.

title: Bespoke Romania experiences
description: When an Angel visits Romania, RUF arranges a curated experience: a program site visit, a dinner with local leadership, a Via Transilvanica segment. Turns a personal trip into a mission moment.

title: Annual personalized year-in-review video
description: Each Angel receives a short personalized video (60-90 seconds) at year-end summarizing their specific impact: the programs their gift funded, the people it reached, what comes next. Higher production value for higher tiers.
